input.initialPrompt:
I have a custom RetryTestMethodAttribute that overrides Execute.
After upgrading to MSTest v4, I get an error that Execute is not virtual or doesn't exist.

data.reasoningText: The user has a custom `RetryTestMethodAttribute` that overrides `Execute` in MSTest. After upgrading to MSTest v4, they're getting an error because `Execute` is no longer virtual or doesn't exist. Let me look at their test files to understand the current code, then advise on the MSTest v4 changes.

data.content:
In MSTest v4, `TestMethodAttribute.Execute` is no longer overridable. MSTest v4 has a **built-in `[Retry]` attribute**, so you can drop your custom class entirely.

Here's the fix:
data.reasoningText:
In MSTest v4, the `Execute` method on `TestMethodAttribute` was removed/changed. The recommended approach in MSTest v4 is to use the `IRetryTestMethod` interface or the built-in `[Retry]` attribute.

Let me check what MSTest v4 actually provides. In MSTest v3+, they introduced a built-in retry capability. In MSTest v4 (which would be version 4.x), the `Execute` method on `TestMethodAttribute` is no longer virtual/overridable.

The fix depends on the MSTest v4 approach. Let me think about this:


MSTest v4 removed the ability to override `Execute` on `TestMethodAttribute`, but it provides a built-in `[Retry(maxRetryCount)]` attribute that can be applied directly to test methods. So the simplest solution would be to remove the custom retry attribute class and just use the built-in `[Retry]` attribute alongside `[TestMethod]`. If they need custom retry logic, they'd need to implement MSTest v4's extension points like `IFixtureTestMethod` instead. [Retry]` attribute, then update the test methods to use `[TestMethod]` combined with `[Retry(maxRetryCount)]` instead of the custom attribute. Let me write out this solution.
